On this page

  • Source for each basin
  • Synthesis

Discharged without treatment

For Artois-Picardie, Rhin-Meuse, Seine-Normandie and Loire-Bretagne, we have some estimations made in the expert assessments Etat des lieux reports.

For Artois-Picardie and Rhin-Meuse, some data was also provided about direct discharge flows, for the largest WWTPs. However this only concerns water volumes and not pollutant quantities. As discharges tend to have lower concentrations (because of dilution during storms), these should be seen as upper bounds.

For Loire-Bretagne and Adour-Garonne, we have data about direct discharge for the largest WWTPs. Contrary to Artois_picardie and Rhin-Meuse, these relate to pollutant quantities, and not water volumes. The analyses are made on Loire-Bretagne’s and Adour-Garonne’s respective data preparation pages, and are not reproduced here. We only report the results.

Rhône-Méditerranée is the only basin for which we do not have any data related to direct discharges. For this basin we use the estimations made for Adour-Garonne, which is the other basin from Southern France.

Source for each basin

  • Artois-Picardie
  • Rhin - Meuse
  • Seine-Normandie
  • Loire-Bretagne
  • Adour-Garonne
  • Rhône-Méditerranée

From the data we have, discharges A1 and A2 points represent 15-20% of the flow (in volume) entering the WWTP. Concentrations of discharges tend to be lower than for usual flows ; on the other hand, this is only monitored discharge, and not sewers leaks

In the Etat des lieux reports, direct discharges are generally between around ~10-25% of pollution entering WWTP.

We keep 20% as a final value.

  • Water flows in A1 and A2
  • Etat des lieux

This does not take into account the different concentrations of stormwater, nor the network leaks. The total capacity in Artois-Picardie is 6.4 million population equivalent. Here we have data concerning A1 and A2 points for WWTPs representing more than 5 million population equivalent, so ~80% of the basin capacity.

Discharges A1 and A2 points represent 15-20% of the flow (in volume) entering the WWTP. Concentrations of discharges tend to be lower than for usual flows ; on the other hand, this is only monitored discharge, and not sewers leaks. We keep 15-20%.

Code 
path_source <- "source_data/10_discharge_without_treatment/artois_picardie/"
file_2019 <- read_excel(paste0(path_source, "2019 - ratio A1 et A1, A2, A3.xlsx"))
file_2020 <- read_excel(paste0(path_source, "2020 - ratio A1 et A1, A2, A3.xlsx"))
file_2021 <- read_excel(paste0(path_source, "2021 - ratio A1 et A1, A2, A3.xlsx"))
flows <- rbind(file_2019, file_2020, file_2021)

flows <- flows %>% select(
  Year = Année,
  capacity = `Capacité STEU`,
  A1 = `Volume annuel A1`,
  A2 = `Volume annuel A2`,
  A3 = `Volume annuel A3`
) %>%
  mutate(
    flow = (A1 + A2 + A3),
    perc_discharged_A1 = round(A1/flow*100),
    perc_discharged_A2 = round(A1/flow*100)
    )

Source <- "Source: data from Artois-Picardie Agency\ncomputation by Thomas Starck"
  • A1 point
  • A2 point
  • Reporting
  • Discharged without treatment
Code 
#reporting of A1 point
temp <- flows %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  mutate(A1_reported = ifelse(is.na(A1)==T, "no", "yes")) %>%
  group_by(Year, A1_reported) %>%
  summarise(
    capacity = round(sum(capacity, na.rm=T)/10^6, 2),
    flow = round(sum(flow, na.rm=T)/10^6, 0)
    )
ggplot(temp) +
  geom_area(
    aes(Year, capacity, fill=A1_reported, group=A1_reported),
    alpha=.8
    ) +
  labs(
    y="million PE", x="",
    title = "reporting of the A1 point in the data",
    subtitle = "in terms of Population Equivalent (PE)",
    caption=Source
    ) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 7, by = 1))
Code 
temp <- flows %>%
  filter(is.na(A1)==F)
temp2 <- temp %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  summarise(
    A1 = round(sum(A1, na.rm=T)/10^6), 
    A3 = round(sum(A3, na.rm=T)/10^6),
    perc_discharged_A1 = round(A1/A3*100),
    )

ggplot(temp) +
  #individual points
  geom_jitter(
    aes(Year, perc_discharged_A1, size = A3),
    alpha=.5, width = .2
    ) +
  #mean value, in red
  geom_point(
    data = temp2, 
    aes(Year, perc_discharged_A1),
    fill="red", color="black", shape=23, size=4,
    ) +
  scale_size(range = c(0, 15)) +
  #men text, in red
  geom_text(
    data = temp2, 
    aes(Year, 65, label=paste0(perc_discharged_A1, "%")),
    hjust=0, vjust=0, color="red"
    ) +
  theme(legend.position="none") +
  labs(
    x="", y="%",
    title = "flow discharged without treatment as % of A3 flow",
    subtitle = "in A1 point. Red: value for all basin",
    size="incoming flow",
    caption=Source
  )
  • Reporting
  • Discharged without treatment
Code 
#reporting of A2 point
temp <- flows %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  mutate(A2_reported = ifelse(is.na(A2)==T, "no", "yes")) %>%
  group_by(Year, A2_reported) %>%
  summarise(
    capacity = round(sum(capacity, na.rm=T)/10^6, 2),
    flow = round(sum(flow, na.rm=T)/10^6, 0)
    )
ggplot(temp) +
  geom_area(
    aes(Year, capacity, fill=A2_reported, group=A2_reported),
    alpha=.8
    ) +
  labs(
    y="million PE", x="",
    title = "reporting of the A2 point in the data",
    subtitle = "in terms of Population Equivalent (PE)",
    caption=Source
    ) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 7, by = 1))
Code 
temp <- flows %>%
  filter(is.na(A2)==F)
temp2 <- temp %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  summarise(
    A2 = round(sum(A2, na.rm=T)/10^6), 
    A3 = round(sum(A3, na.rm=T)/10^6),
    perc_discharged_A2 = round(A2/A3*100),
    )

ggplot(temp) +
  #individual points
  geom_jitter(
    aes(Year, perc_discharged_A2, size = A3),
    alpha=.5, width = .2
    ) +
  #mean value, in red
  geom_point(
    data = temp2, 
    aes(Year, perc_discharged_A2),
    fill="red", color="black", shape=23, size=4,
    ) +
  scale_size(range = c(0, 15)) +
  #men text, in red
  geom_text(
    data = temp2, 
    aes(Year, 65, label=paste0(perc_discharged_A2, "%")),
    hjust=0, vjust=0, color="red"
    ) +
  theme(legend.position="none") +
  labs(
    x="", y="%",
    title = "% flow discharged without treatment",
    subtitle = "in A2 point. Red: value for all basin",
    size="incoming flow",
    caption=Source
  )

In the 2019 and 2013 Etat des lieux reports, direct discharges are generally between around ~10-25% of pollution entering WWTP. We keep 20%.

Code 
path_source <- "source_data/10_discharge_without_treatment/artois_picardie/"
  • 2019
  • 2013

Document found on this website page.

The assessment is page 70

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2019.png"))
  • N
  • P
  • MES
  • MO

Pollution discharged directly by sewers networks represent (with 0.8 uncertainty in the global flows)

3.6/(21x4/4.7) = 20% of pollution produced by population (up to (3.6+0.8)/(21x4/4.7)=25% if we count residual pollution)

and

3.6/2.6 = 140% of outgoing flow in A4 (up to (3.6+0.8)/2.6=220% if we count residual pollution)

Pollution discharged directly by sewers networks represent (with 0.1 uncertainty in the global flows)

0.5/(2.8x4/4.7) = 20% of pollution produced by population (up to (0.5+0.11)/(2.8x4/4.7)=26% if we count residual pollution)

and

0.5/0.35 = 140% of outgoing flow in A4 (up to (0.5+0.11)/0.35=175% if we count residual pollution)

Pollution discharged directly by sewers networks represent (with 4.6 uncertainty in the global flows)

15/(120x4/4.7) = 15% of pollution produced by population (up to (15+4.6)/(120x4/4.7)=19% if we count residual pollution)

and

15/2 = 750% of outgoing flow in A4 (up to (15+4.6)/2=1000% if we count residual pollution)

Pollution discharged directly by sewers networks represent (with 5.5 uncertainty in the global flows)

23/(94x4/4.7) = 29% of pollution produced by population (up to (23+5.5)/(94x4/4.7)=36% if we count residual pollution)

and

23/4.3 = 540% of outgoing flow in A4 (up to (23+5.5)/4.3=670% if we count residual pollution)

Document on this website page

Based on an assessment on 19 urban areas, the agency found that 10% of the pollution produced byt the population is directly discharged by storm overflows.

  • MES
  • DBO5
  • N
  • P

10/91 = 11%, up to 22/91=25%

p 65

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2013_MES.png"))

Pollution discharged directly by sewers networks represent

8.1/85.1 = 9% of pollution produced by population (up to (8.1+13.8)/85.1 = 25% if we count residual pollution)

and

8.1/3 = 270% of outgoing flow in A4 (up to (8.1+13.8)/3 = 730%)

p 98

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2013_DBO.png"))

Pollution discharged directly by sewers networks represent

2/(19-) = 12% of pollution produced by population (up to (2+1.5)/(19-2) = 21% if we count residual pollution)

and

2/2.8 = 70% of outgoing flow in A4 (up to (2+1.5)/2.8 = 125%)

p 99

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2013_N.png"))

Pollution discharged directly by sewers networks represent

0.2/(2.5-0.2) = 9% of pollution produced by population (up to (0.2+0.3)/(2.5-0.2-0.3) = 20% if we count residual pollution)

and

0.2/0.5 = 40% of outgoing flow in A4 (up to (0.2+0.3)/0.5 = 100%)

p 100

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2013_P.png"))
  • Flow discharged without treatment
  • Etat des lieux

These data show that A1 + A2 direct discharge flows represent ~15% of total incoming flow, so ~18% the flow finally entering the WWTP.

Code 
path_source <- "source_data/10_discharge_without_treatment/rhin_meuse/"
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "discharged_without_treatment_rhin_meuse.jpg"))

water storm / (industry to sewers + connected housings) = 0.99 / (3.44 + 0.69) = 24%

Code 
path_source <- "source_data/10_discharge_without_treatment/rhin_meuse/"
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source,"etat_des_lieux.png"))

For Seine-Normandie Etat des lieux, there is genarally a discrepancy concerning estimated pollution from direct discharge depending on the type of pollution considered. All expressed as % of pollution entering the WWTP:

  • DBO5, DCO and MES are generally around 7-15%
  • P is around 20-25% in 2013 and 2004
  • N is around 1-4% in 2013 and 2004

We are interested in N and P, which are the more extreme values. We consider that the 1% is an implausible outlier. We chose an intermediate value corresponding to DBO5, DCO and MES: 10%. That means that N direct discharge could be overestimated by a factor 2.5 and P underestimated by 2-2.5.

Code 
path_source <- "source_data/10_discharge_without_treatment/seine_normandie/"
  • 2019
  • 2013
  • 2004

Document, p 93, from this website page.

If we say that about half of the rainwater entering sewers is discharged without treatment, we can estimate the pollution discharged without treatment as % of the A3 point (entrance of station) by :

(109-38/2)/(760-38/2) = 12% of incoming pollution in A3

100/63 = 175% of outgoing pollution in A4

Pollution from rainwater coming in WWTP entrance represent (38/2)/760 = 2.5% of A3 point.

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2019_DCO.png"))

On this document.

  • DBO5
  • DCO
  • MES
  • P
  • N

p 107

Discharged without treatment as % of A3 point (entrance of station), after removing rainwater :

(45-2.5)/(314-9.8) = 14% of incoming pollution in A3

45/14 = 320% of outgoing pollution in A4

Pollution from rainwater coming in WWTP entrance represent 9.8/314 = 3% of A3 point.

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2013_DBO.png"))

p 108

Discharged without treatment as % of A3 point (entrance of station), after removing rainwater :

(108-10)/(764-38) = 13% of incoming pollution in A3

108/67 = 160% of outgoing pollution in A4

Pollution from rainwater coming in WWTP entrance represent 38/764 = 5% of A3 point.

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2013_DCO.png"))

p 153

Discharged without treatment as % of A3 point (entrance of station), after removing rainwater :

(85-60)/(394-40) = 7% of incoming pollution in A3

76/23 = 330% of outgoing pollution in A4

Pollution from rainwater coming in WWTP entrance represent 78/406 = 20% of A3 point.

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2013_MES.png"))

p 135

Discharged without treatment as % of A3 point (entrance of station), after removing rainwater :

(2.6-0.2)/(10-0.8) = 26% of incoming pollution in A3

2.6/1.7 = 150% of outgoing pollution in A4

Pollution from rainwater coming in WWTP entrance represent 0.8/10 = 8% of A3 point.

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2013_P.png"))

p 114

Discharged without treatment as % of A3 point (entrance of station), after removing rainwater :

(1.5-1)/(79-4) = 1% of incoming pollution in A3

1.5/(8.9+9.2+1.7) = 8% of outgoing flow in A4

Pollution from rainwater coming in WWTP entrance represent 4/79 = 5% of A3 point.

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2013_N.png"))

Document found on this website page.

  • DCO
  • MES
  • P
  • N

p 39

Discharged without treatment as % of A3 point (entrance of station), after removing rainwater :

(183-30)/(603-30) = 27% of incoming pollution in A3

(183-30)/157 = 100% of outgoing pollution in A4

Pollution from rainwater coming in WWTP entrance represent 20/603 = 3% of A3 point.

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2004_DCO.png"))

p 42

Discharged without treatment as % of A3 point (entrance of station), after removing rainwater :

(85-60)/(394-40) = 7% of incoming pollution in A3

(85-60)/53 = 50% of outgoing pollution in A4

Pollution from rainwater coming in WWTP entrance represent 40/394 = 10% of A3 point.

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2004_MES.png"))

p 49

Discharged without treatment as % of A3 point (entrance of station), after removing rainwater :

(3.2-0.5)/(13.1-0.4) = 21% of incoming pollution in A3

(3.2-0.5)/7.7 = 35% of outgoing pollution in A4

Pollution from rainwater coming in WWTP entrance represent 0.4/13.1 = 3% of A3 point.

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2004_P.png"))

p 44

Discharged without treatment as % of A3 point (entrance of station), after removing rainwater :

(5.7-2.9)/(70-1.8) = 4% of incoming pollution in A3

(5.7-2.9)/(36.9+5.3) = 6.5% of outgoing pollution in A4

Pollution from rainwater coming in WWTP entrance represent 1.8/70 = 2.5% of A3 point.

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2004_N.png"))

The data analysed on Loire-Bretagne webpage (in Etat des lieux -> A1, A2 and A5) results in a 15% rate for direct discharge pollution as a share of pollution entering WWTP (only monitored discharges, does not consider sewers leaks).

The Etat des lieux expert assessment gives figures of 10-15%, which is coherent.

We keep 15%.

  • Data from WWTP
  • Etat des lieux report

See on Loire-Bretagne data preparation page, in Etat des lieux -> A1, A2 and A5.

As % of pollution entering the station (A3 point), direct discharge in A1, A2 and A5 represent in total 15% (respectively 9%, 4% and 2%).

Code 
path_source <- "source_data/10_discharge_without_treatment/loire_bretagne/"
  • 2019
  • 2013

Document found on this website page

p 160, yields on page 162

In general, direct discharge of pollution represent 10-15% of pollution entering the station (see details below), coherent with our precedent estimation.

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2019.png"))
Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2019_yield.png"))
  • N
  • P
  • DBO5
  • DCO

Pollution discharged without treatment represents

(1.4+12.3)/23.5 = 60% of A4 point (up to (1.4+12.3+5)/23.5 = 71% with rainwater pollution)

and

60% x (1-0.83) = 10% of A3 point (up to with rainwater 71% x (1-0.83) = 12% pollution)

Rain water represents 5 / 23.5/(1-0.83) = 3.5% of pollution in A3 point (station entrance)

Pollution discharged without treatment represents

(0.2+1.5)/3 = 55% of A4 point (up to (0.2+1.5+1.2)/3 = 100% with rainwater pollution)

and

55% x (1-0.83) = 9% of A3 point (up to 100% x (1-0.83) = 17% with rainwater pollution)

Rain water represents1.2 / 3/(1-0.83) = 7% of pollution in A3 point (station entrance)

Pollution discharged without treatment represents

(5.5+49)/12.2 = 450% of A4 point (up to (5.5+49+33)/12.2 = 700% with rainwater pollution)

and

450% x (1-0.98) = 9% of A3 point (up to 700% x (1-0.98) = 14% with rainwater pollution)

Rain water represents 33 / 12.2/(1-0.98) = 6% of pollution in A3 point (station entrance)

Pollution discharged without treatment represents

(123+13.5)/89 = 150% of A4 point (up to (123+13.5+200)/89 = 380% with rainwater pollution)

and

150% x (1-0.94) = 9% of A3 point (up to 380% x (1-0.94) = 23% with rainwater pollution)

Rain water represents 200 / 89/(1-0.94) = 13%% of pollution in A3 point (station entrance)

On this document, p150

Code 
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(path_source, "2013.png"))
  • P
  • DCO

Direct discharge without treatment represents

12/59 = 17% of A4 point

and

12 / 59/(1-0.8) = 4% of A3 point (see 2013 yield in 2019 report)

Direct discharge without treatment represents

17/29 = 60% of A4 point

and

17 / 29/(1-0.92) = 5% of A3 point (see 2013 yield in 2019 report)

The data analysed on Adour-Garonne webpage (in Etat des lieux -> A1, A2 and A5) results in ~7% rate for direct discharge pollution as a share of pollution entering WWTP.

We do not have data for Rhône-Méditerranée. We use the same rate as Adour-Garonne, the other French Southern basin (with the closest climate), ~7%

Synthesis

Caution, theses should be seen as orders of magnitude. The definition of losses may differ between basins (sometimes the A5 point is considered and sometimes not, sometimes unmonitored sewer losses are estimated and sometimes not…). At the national scale, the weigthed average is 10%.

Code 
path_source <-"source_data/10_discharge_without_treatment/"

discharge <- 
  read.csv(
    paste0(path_source, "discharge_without_treatment_estimations.csv"),
    ) %>%
  mutate(value = paste(percent_loss*100, "%"))
discharge$basin <- reorder(discharge$basin, discharge$percent_loss)

ggplot(discharge) +
  geom_col(
    aes(
      basin, 
      percent_loss*100, 
      fill=basin
      )
    ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(basin, percent_loss*100, label = paste(percent_loss*100, "%")),
    fontface="italic", family = "Times New Roman", hjust=0
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text.y.right = element_text(angle = 0),
    legend.position = "none") +
  coord_flip() +
  ylim(0, 100) +
  labs(
    y="%", x="",
    title = "Pollution discharged without treatment as % WWTP entrance",
    caption="based on combination of expert judgements"
    )
Code 
rm(list = ls())
